fields.description:
## Why
`test_completion_floor_e2e.py::test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only` (`:178-198`) asserts,
via `inspect.getsource` over the floor functions, that tokens like `sign_manifest`,
`transition`, `reopen(` are ABSENT from their source, and that `completion_floor` does not
appear in `rebar.llm.completion`'s source. It is a pure change-detector: a rename, a helper,
or an indirect call evades it, and it exercises no runtime behavior. VALIDATED.

Note: `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` is a PURE verdict→verdict transform (no `repo_root`,
no store/LLM I/O), so its "no side effects" cannot be shown by a store snapshot — there is no
I/O pathway to observe. The behavioral replacement therefore tests two things that ARE
observable against the current code: the floor's real transform contract, and the confinement
of the floor to the plan-review path (that the close/verify verdict handler never invokes it).

## What
Replace the source-token test with two behavioral assertions in the same file:

1. **Floor transform + conservation (its real, observable contract).** Run
   `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` over the file's existing gold fixture in a case that
   DROPS a finding, and assert on the returned verdict: (i) the expected finding moved from
   `advisory` to `dropped`; (ii) findings are CONSERVED — the set of finding ids across
   `advisory ∪ dropped` after equals the set before (the floor only re-partitions; it never
   creates, loses, or rewrites a finding); (iii) the floor adds NO signing/attestation output —
   the returned verdict grows no `signature` / `attestation` / `attestations` key (its only
   effect is the advisory/dropped/coverage partition). This is non-vacuous: it exercises the
   floor's real drop decision and would fail if the floor did anything beyond re-partitioning.

2. **Confinement: the close/verify verdict handler does NOT invoke the floor.** Install spies
   (monkeypatch counters) on `plan_review._maybe_apply_completion_floor` AND
   `plan_review._apply_completion_floor_to_verdict`, then run the deterministic close-path
   verdict handler `rebar.llm.completion.reconcile_verdict(...)` over a canned
   `completion_verdict`, and assert NEITHER floor spy was called. This runs the REAL close-path
   verdict normalization and behaviorally proves the plan-review floor is not wired into the
   completion/close path — the guard the old `getsource(close_path)` check crudely approximated,
   but now robust to renames/indirect calls (the spy is on the actual floor binding).

3. Keep the genuine artifact-contract assertion `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"`
   (no NEW attestation kind introduced) — that is a real invariant, not a source grep.

Delete the `inspect.getsource` token-absence assertions for the floor functions and for
`rebar.llm.completion`.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_completion_floor_e2e.py` — replace `test_non_regression_floor_is_review_only`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A behavioral test runs the floor over the gold fixture and asserts the finding is dropped,
      findings are conserved (no id created/lost), and NO signing/attestation key is added to
      the returned verdict.
- [ ] A behavioral test spies both floor entry points and runs `completion.reconcile_verdict`,
      asserting the floor is NEVER invoked by that close-path handler.
- [ ] No `inspect.getsource` token-absence assertion for the floor functions remains; the
      `attest._MANIFEST_PREFIX == "plan-review"` artifact contract is retained.
- [ ] The confinement test would fail if `reconcile_verdict` were made to call the floor —
      verified by a mutation check (temporarily add the call; confirm RED).
- [ ] `make test` green.
